behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff between evaluate-stage command logic and artifact persistence. A unit test of slot-selection logic alone can miss wiring mistakes in artifact writer integration. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is plumbing-only ("stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact"), so no real OS/network/subprocess requirement (not System), no model-output quality check (not Agentic), and no multi-agentic sequence (not Workflow). IA-UA demotion proof: quoted text checks destination slot routing only, not grounding quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with a deterministic fixture input in a local in-process run context; assert exactly one new/updated `grounding` artifact is written and `plan` is unchanged by this step.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state handoff (artifact read + stage execution + artifact write). Unit is too low because bugs often occur in wiring/order between reader and writer modules. Rejected higher levels: text is explicit plumbing ("reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`") and does not require asserting model judgment quality; no real infra dependency is stated; no multiple agentic decisions required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage through normal orchestrator with deterministic stage stub, assert grounding is consumed (e.g., included in stage input context) and output is written to `plan` while `grounding` remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is conditional behavior across issue-content reader + deep-dive stage + artifact writer. Unit tests can miss side effects in wiring. Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on side-effect suppression ("no longer overwrites `plan`") driven by existing issue diagnosis, not LLM output correctness; no stated need for real OS/network/subprocess; not a multi-agentic pipeline assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare issue content containing diagnosis and preexisting `plan`; execute deep-dive stage via integrated local harness; assert no write/overwrite occurs to `plan` and existing artifact contents/version remain unchanged.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: This is persistence/lineage correctness across stages in one run context, which is an inter-module contract (context store + stage resolver + implement reader). Unit is too low because lineage bugs are often in serialization/lookup boundaries. Rejected higher levels: text targets lineage plumbing ("implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier") not model-quality assertions; no explicit real infra requirement; sequence is multi-stage but can be validated without real model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate-stage to write `grounding` in a local run context, then execute implement-stage in the same context ID; assert implement resolves the exact prior artifact lineage reference and reads expected payload.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end multi-step agent pipeline and asserts downstream output reflects earlier grounding. This crosses multiple agentic handoffs; a mocked/stubbed model can hide reflection failures caused by real model decisions. Rejected lower levels: description itself is "full multi-step agent run" and "final implementation plan reflects grounding," which disqualifies Integration/Agentic-single-step-only approaches.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real chain end-to-end in one run context (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`) with representative issue input; assert final `plan` content materially incorporates facts from produced `grounding` artifact and trace shows stage-to-stage carryover.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback orchestration with artifact store interactions (missing-read path + write path + non-target artifact integrity). Unit is too low because corruption/regression appears at module boundaries. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is resilience/plumbing ("falls back safely" / "still emits a valid plan artifact" / "without corrupting other stored artifacts") and does not require evaluating model output quality; no explicit real infra dependency; no multi-agentic sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage in context with no `grounding` artifact but with other artifacts present; assert `plan` is produced, no exceptions from missing grounding, and all non-plan artifacts remain byte-for-byte/version unchanged.
